title: Fix Vite dev server proxy connection refused (ECONNREFUSED) on localhost:3000
[Feature] Core User Authentication (GitHub/Google OAuth & Workspace Isolation)
description:
# [Feature] Core User Authentication (GitHub/Google OAuth & Workspace Isolation)

This issue tracks the design and implementation of core user authentication using GitHub & Google OAuth, along with establishing strict, multi-tenant workspace isolation across the API and web applications.

## 👥 Personas
- **The Developer (Individual Contributor)**: Frictionless onboarding without managing new passwords.
- **The Organization Owner (Workspace Admin)**: Complete confidence in tenant isolation and data protection.
- **The Cross-Functional Collaborator (Agile Coach)**: Fluid switching between multiple workspaces.

## 🎯 User Stories (JTBD)
- **US1: OAuth Sign-In & Automated Account Provisioning**: Sign in via GitHub/Google OAuth, automatically provision user profiles, and establish secure session cookies.
- **US2: Active Workspace Selection & Context Switching**: Dynamically switch workspaces via sidebar dropdown and update the active route context.
- **US3: Strict Tenant Isolation & Data Leakage Prevention**: Isolate all database queries, API endpoints, and event logs by workspace ID.
- **US4: Secure Workspace Invitation & Membership Flow**: Generate secure, HMAC-signed invitation links to onboard new members seamlessly.

## 🚦 Functional Requirements
- **FR9**: Standard integration-tested OAuth 2.0 flows for GitHub and Google.
- **FR10**: Atomic user profile creation in a single transaction upon successful login.
- **FR11**: Session tokens issued as secure, `HttpOnly`, `SameSite=Lax`, `__Host-` prefixed cookies.
- **FR12**: `GET /api/auth/me` endpoint to fetch authenticated user metadata.
- **FR13**: `GET /api/workspaces` endpoint to return authorized workspaces for active users.
- **FR14**: Backend middleware `TenantGuard` to intercept and check workspace-member associations.
- **FR15**: SQL queries explicitly scoping operations by `workspace_id`.
- **FR16**: Standard `404 Not Found` returned on unauthorized cross-tenant requests.
- **FR17-FR20**: Invitation URL creation, HMAC verification, membership database insertion, and single-use link consumption.

## 🛠️ Architectural Direction
- **Session Strategy**: Private Session Cookies backed by server-side PostgreSQL storage. Better XSS protection than local storage JWTs and allows instant revocation.
- **Credential Fallback**: Argon2id password hashing for non-federated logins, evaluated using non-blocking Tokio threads.
- **Isolation Enforcement**: Path-based routing scoping combined with Axum Extractors (`WorkspaceContext`) to verify tenant membership and enforce database query parameterization.

---
See the full discovery document in the repository at [docs/product/v0.2_auth_discovery.md](file:///Users/fernandoike/projects/pets/kanbrio/docs/product/v0.2_auth_discovery.md).

title: [Feature] Core User Authentication (GitHub/Google OAuth & Workspace Isolation)
description:
# [Feature] Core User Authentication (GitHub/Google OAuth & Workspace Isolation)

This issue tracks the design and implementation of core user authentication using GitHub & Google OAuth, along with establishing strict, multi-tenant workspace isolation across the API and web applications.

## 👥 Personas
- **The Developer (Individual Contributor)**: Frictionless onboarding without managing new passwords.
- **The Organization Owner (Workspace Admin)**: Complete confidence in tenant isolation and data protection.
- **The Cross-Functional Collaborator (Agile Coach)**: Fluid switching between multiple workspaces.

## 🎯 User Stories (JTBD)
- **US1: OAuth Sign-In & Automated Account Provisioning**: Sign in via GitHub/Google OAuth, automatically provision user profiles, and establish secure session cookies.
- **US2: Active Workspace Selection & Context Switching**: Dynamically switch workspaces via sidebar dropdown and update the active route context.
- **US3: Strict Tenant Isolation & Data Leakage Prevention**: Isolate all database queries, API endpoints, and event logs by workspace ID.
- **US4: Secure Workspace Invitation & Membership Flow**: Generate secure, HMAC-signed invitation links to onboard new members seamlessly.

## 🚦 Functional Requirements
- **FR9**: Standard integration-tested OAuth 2.0 flows for GitHub and Google.
- **FR10**: Atomic user profile creation in a single transaction upon successful login.
- **FR11**: Session tokens issued as secure, `HttpOnly`, `SameSite=Lax`, `__Host-` prefixed cookies.
- **FR12**: `GET /api/auth/me` endpoint to fetch authenticated user metadata.
- **FR13**: `GET /api/workspaces` endpoint to return authorized workspaces for active users.
- **FR14**: Backend middleware `TenantGuard` to intercept and check workspace-member associations.
- **FR15**: SQL queries explicitly scoping operations by `workspace_id`.
- **FR16**: Standard `404 Not Found` returned on unauthorized cross-tenant requests.
- **FR17-FR20**: Invitation URL creation, HMAC verification, membership database insertion, and single-use link consumption.

## 🛠️ Architectural Direction
- **Session Strategy**: Private Session Cookies backed by server-side PostgreSQL storage. Better XSS protection than local storage JWTs and allows instant revocation.
- **Credential Fallback**: Argon2id password hashing for non-federated logins, evaluated using non-blocking Tokio threads.
- **Isolation Enforcement**: Path-based routing scoping combined with Axum Extractors (`WorkspaceContext`) to verify tenant membership and enforce database query parameterization.

---
See the full discovery document in the repository at [docs/product/v0.2_auth_discovery.md](file:///Users/fernandoike/projects/pets/kanbrio/docs/product/v0.2_auth_discovery.md).

title: [Feature] Flow Efficiency & Aging WIP metrics
description:
## Description
Provide strategic efficiency metrics including Flow Efficiency (ratio of active work time vs queue/idle time) and an Aging WIP radar to highlight stagnant tasks before they breach SLA targets.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Automatically calculate Flow Efficiency: `(Total Lead Time - Total Queue Time) / Total Lead Time`.
- Highlight active cards spending an abnormal amount of time in a single stage (Aging WIP).
- Present simple, visually compelling metrics dashboards on the frontend.

title: [Feature] Cycle Time Scatter Plot
description:
## Description
Implement a Cycle Time Scatter Plot to visualize individual card cycle times, identify delivery outliers, and calculate percentile SLA thresholds (e.g. 50%, 85%, 95% cycle time predictability).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Retrieve absolute lead/cycle times from creation to completion for all resolved cards.
- Display completed cards as points on a scatter plot where Y-axis represents cycle time in days and X-axis is the completion date.
- Draw percentile horizontal indicators (50%, 70%, 85%, 95% thresholds).

title: [Feature] Cumulative Flow Diagram (CFD)
description:
## Description
Create a Cumulative Flow Diagram (CFD) to visualize the stability of the workflow, monitor WIP trends, and easily identify systemic bottlenecks over time.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Compute active card counts per column/stage historically using the audit and transition event logs.
- Render a stacked area chart on the frontend (using Apache ECharts) showing columns/stages over time.
- Allow filtering by date range, swimlanes, and workspace.

title: [Feature] Monte Carlo Simulation Engine
description:
## Description
Build a probabilistic forecasting engine using historical card throughput data (Monte Carlo simulations) to enable data-driven project delivery projections and predictability.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Implement 'When' simulations: Provide a probabilistic date forecast for a specific backlog completion (e.g. 85% confidence for Date X).
- Implement 'How Many' simulations: Predict throughput/completed cards count for a fixed time box.
- Extract historical cycle time and throughput metrics from the database's immutable event transition log.
- Present the simulation results dynamically on the frontend using robust visualizations (e.g., Apache ECharts).

title: [Feature] User WIP Limits
description:
## Description
Implement User-level Work-in-Progress (WIP) limits to prevent team member burnout and improve focus by restricting the number of active, uncompleted cards assigned to an individual team member.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Add a `wip_limit` configuration field for users/workspace members.
- Validate during card creation or movement (assignment changes) that a user does not exceed their active WIP limit.
- Return a transactional `WipLimitExceeded` error from the API (409 Conflict) if the limit is breached.
- Show custom shake animations and toast notifications on the UI when a user WIP limit is exceeded.
- Add automated unit/integration and E2E tests for user WIP limits.

title: [Design] Transition & Blocker Visual Language
description: Implement the visual representation of card states (specifically 'Blocked') and provide a UI to view the card transition audit log. This includes adding block/unblock actions and a history sidebar. Part of Cycle 1 completion.

title: [Feature] Real-time Sync with WebSockets
description: Implement instant board updates across all clients using native WebSockets. Part of Cycle 2: Advanced Workflow & Automation.

title: [Feature] Business Rules Engine (IFTTT status sync)
description: Develop a rules engine to automate workflow actions, such as automatically moving a parent card when all children are finished. Part of Cycle 2: Advanced Workflow & Automation.

title: [Feature] Arrival & Departure Rules (Checklists & Validation)
description: Implement mandatory constraints for card movement. This includes checklists that must be completed before a card can leave a column and field validation upon entry/exit. Part of Cycle 2: Advanced Workflow & Automation.

title: [Feature] Transition Auditing (Event Logging)
description:
## Objective
Implement comprehensive Transition Auditing (Event Logging) for Kanban cards to track all lifecycle events with detailed context.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. **Event Capture**: Every state change on a card MUST trigger an immutable record in the `card_transitions` table.
2. **Supported Event Types**: `create`, `move`, `block`, `unblock`, `edit`, `archive`, `delete`.
3. **Data Schema**: Each record MUST include `card_id`, `user_id` (actor), `transition_type`, from/to column/swimlane IDs, and a `payload` JSONB field for event-specific data (e.g., property changes).
4. **Transactional Integrity**: Card state changes and event logging MUST occur within the same database transaction.
5. **Security**: The history retrieval API MUST enforce workspace-level access control.
6. **Immutability**: Audit records MUST be append-only.

## Technical Strategy
- Hybrid approach: UUID columns for metrics + JSONB payload for flexibility.
- Model update in `apps/api/src/models/audit.rs`.
- Transactional logic in `apps/api/src/models/card.rs`.

title: [Infra] End-to-End (E2E) Testing Foundation with Playwright
description:
### Goal
Establish the End-to-End testing infrastructure using Playwright to validate critical user flows, starting with the Drag & Drop functionality.

### Context
With the Drag & Drop engine integrated and the local development environment stabilized with deterministic seeds, we need automated visual/interaction tests that unit tests cannot cover.

### Tasks
- [ ] Initialize Playwright in the monorepo (likely under `apps/web` or a dedicated `e2e` package).
- [ ] Configure Playwright to use the local development setup (using the deterministic `scripts/seed.sql`).
- [ ] Write the first E2E test: **Card Movement** (Drag a card from 'To Do' to 'Doing', assert UI change, refresh page, assert persistence).
- [ ] Add `make test-e2e` to the root `Makefile`.

title: [Infra] Local Dev Environment & Data Seeding
description:
### Goal
Provide a seamless way to start the local development environment with sample data for visual testing.

### Tasks
- Create a `docker-compose.yml` for local PostgreSQL.
- Implement a `seed` script (or SQL file) to create a default Workspace, Columns, Swimlanes, and Cards.
- Document the startup process in `CONTRIBUTING.md` or `README.md`.

### Context
Required to verify Issue #21 (Drag & Drop) and future UI features visually.

title: [Feature] Drag & Drop Engine Integration
description:
### Context
Cards must be movable across the 2D grid with extreme performance.

### Tasks
- Integrate @atlaskit/pragmatic-drag-and-drop into the Board components.
- Implement drag logic (Start, Update, Drop).
- On Drop, call the backend Card::move_to API.
- Implement optimistic UI updates (moving the card instantly before the API confirms, reverting on 409 WipLimitExceeded).

### Acceptance Criteria
- Users can physically drag a card between columns/swimlanes, and the backend updates correctly (verifiable via the audit log).

Cycle 2: The Reactive Board

title: [Feature] 2D Board Layout & State Fetching
description:
### Context
The board must render Columns and Swimlanes based on the backend data.

### Tasks
- Create Board, Column, and Swimlane layout components.
- Integrate @tanstack/solid-query to fetch the initial state from the Rust backend (BoardState::get_state).
- Render the fetched Columns, Swimlanes, and populate them with the Card components.

### Acceptance Criteria
- The UI displays a grid with real data from the PostgreSQL database (read-only mode).

Cycle 2: The Reactive Board

title: [Feature] Card Component & Visual States
description:
### Context
The card is the atomic unit of Kanban. It needs to reflect the tokens defined in Issue #10.

### Tasks
- Create the Card SolidJS component.
- Implement the UI for the Header (Breadcrumbs), Body (Title, Blocker Reason), and Footer (Metadata).
- Implement visual states: Default, Blocked, Delayed (consuming the CSS variables from ui-core).

### Acceptance Criteria
- Storybook/Test page renders cards in various states accurately matching DESIGN.md.

Cycle 2: The Reactive Board

title: [Infra] Initialize SolidStart Workspace
description:
### Context
apps/web is currently an empty shell. We need a modern, fast development environment.

### Tasks
- Initialize a SolidStart (or Solid + Vite) application in apps/web.
- Configure Tailwind CSS to consume packages/ui-core/tailwind.config.js.
- Set up routing and the main layout shell.

### Acceptance Criteria
- 'npm run dev' in apps/web displays a 'Hello Kanbrio' screen with Tailwind styling applied.

Cycle 2: The Reactive Board

title: [Design] Transition & Blocker Visual Language
description:
**Context:** Every move is an event. We need clear visual feedback for card transitions and blockers to support flow analysis.
**Value:** Improves user awareness of flow status and bottlenecks.

**Acceptance Criteria (AC):**
- Define Motion Tokens (easings and durations) for card movements inspired by Material 3 Expressive.
- Design visual states for 'Blocked', 'Unblocked', and 'Delayed' cards.
- Specify accessibility labels and screen reader feedback for state transitions.

title: [Design] Core UI Foundation & Design System Tokens
description:
**Context:** Establish the foundational visual tokens based on DESIGN.md to ensure consistency across the monorepo.
**Value:** Prevents visual drift and provides a single source of truth for the @developer.

**Acceptance Criteria (AC):**
- Translate DESIGN.md tokens into Tailwind/CSS variables within packages/ui-core.
- Document typography scales and spacing systems in the design manifest.
- Ensure all tokens meet WCAG AA accessibility standards.

title: [Design] 2D Kanban Board & Hierarchy Visualization
description:
**Context:** Kanbrio core value is its infinite hierarchy within a 2D board. We need a layout that is dense but readable.
**User Story:** As a Project Manager, I want to see how child tasks roll up to parent initiatives within columns and swimlanes.

**Acceptance Criteria (AC):**
- Define the visual representation for Parent-Child relationships on a card.
- Create the layout specification for nested columns and swimlanes in DESIGN.md.
- Validate the layout for high-density data scenarios (many cards/swimlanes).

title: [Infra] PR and Issue Templates
description:
**Context:** Our architecture requires specific agent audits (`@security`, `@sre`, `@legal-counsel`). PR templates force authors to document that they passed these processes.
**Value:** Standardization of the Definition of Done (DoD) and reduction of cognitive load during code review.

**Acceptance Criteria (AC):**
- [ ] `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` file created.
- [ ] Template includes a mandatory checklist for AI Audits (Security, SRE, Legal).
- [ ] Template includes a section to reference the original Issue ("Closes #X").
- [ ] `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/feature_request.md` created to standardize the User Story format.

title: [Infra] Basic CI Pipeline (GitHub Actions)
description:
**Context:** We need a fast, automated feedback loop. The developer (or agent) needs to know in minutes if their code broke something.
**Value:** Reduction of bugs in production, code style consistency, and continuous validation of monorepo health.

**Acceptance Criteria (AC):**
- [ ] `.github/workflows/ci.yml` file created.
- [ ] Backend Job: Runs `cargo clippy` (no warnings allowed), `cargo fmt`, and `cargo test`.
- [ ] Frontend Job: Prepares the Node/Bun environment for future tests and linters.
- [ ] Pipeline runs automatically on every Push to PRs and `main`.

title: [Infra] Configure Branch Protections
description:
**Context:** To adopt the "Agentic Workflow", we must ensure no code goes to production without passing Quality Gates and AI audits. The `main` branch must be immutable directly.
**Value:** Prevent breaking the production environment and ensure reviews (human and AI) are always respected.

**Acceptance Criteria (AC):**
- [ ] Direct `push` to `main` branch blocked in GitHub.
- [ ] Pull Request (PR) requirement enabled.
- [ ] At least 1 review approval required before merge.
- [ ] Status checks marked as required (PR cannot be merged if CI fails).
- [ ] Linear history required (Squash and Merge or Rebase).

title: [Feature] Multi-level WIP Limits
description:
**Context:** Limiting Work In Progress (WIP) is fundamental to Kanban. The system must actively prevent overload.
**User Story:** As an Agile Coach, I want to set a maximum limit of cards for a specific column, to prevent the team from starting more work than they can finish.

**Acceptance Criteria (AC):**
- [ ] Backend logic developed to count cards in the destination column before authorizing a transition.
- [ ] If a transition exceeds the configured WIP Limit for that column/swimlane, the API must return a relevant HTTP error (e.g., 409 Conflict or 422 Unprocessable Entity).

title: [Feature] Transition Auditing (Event Logging)
description:
**Context:** To allow the `@kanban-expert` to calculate metrics (Monte Carlo, Lead Time), we need the exact history of every card movement.
**User Story:** As an Agile Coach, I want the system to record the exact timestamp of a card movement, so I can identify flow bottlenecks.

**Acceptance Criteria (AC):**
- [ ] Immutable `card_transitions` table created.
- [ ] Columns: `card_id`, `from_column_id`, `to_column_id`, `occurred_at`.
- [ ] Atomic transactions in the Backend (Rust) ensuring that changing a card's column **always** writes the corresponding log.

title: [Feature] 2D Context (Columns x Swimlanes)
description:
**Context:** Kanbrio is a 2D board. Each task must exist at the intersection of a Status (Column) and a Category (Swimlane).
**User Story:** As a Project Manager, I want to see tasks organized horizontally (e.g., Expedite) and vertically (e.g., Doing), to prioritize urgent work without losing workflow visibility.

**Acceptance Criteria (AC):**
- [ ] `cards` table has `current_column_id` (UUID, Not Null).
- [ ] `cards` table has `current_swimlane_id` (UUID, Not Null).

title: [Feature] Recursive Task Structure (Hierarchy)
description:
**Context:** Traditional tools limit hierarchy. Our differentiator is supporting infinite trees using the Adjacency List pattern.
**User Story:** As a Project Manager, I want to create initiatives and attach infinite levels of child tasks, so I can see how granular work contributes to the high-level roadmap.

**Acceptance Criteria (AC):**
- [ ] `cards` table created in PostgreSQL with a `parent_id` (UUID) column pointing to itself.
- [ ] Initial SQL migration created.
- [ ] Ensure deleting a "Parent" correctly handles "Children" (Cascade or Soft-delete).
